_meta.title: Elegoo SDCP (Centauri Carbon 1) — the TWO status enums (machine + job)
_meta.confidence: 🟡 source-read (Elegoo first-party SDK); 🔵 SDCP path community firmware-validated

derived_from.section: Reading state — SDCP path (CC1): two enums, both load-bearing
derived_from.source_note: Clean-room from `elegoo-link` + SDCP v3.0 spec. FDM firmware repurposes the resin status enum.

notes: ⚠️ TWO distinct enums live on the SAME frame — keep them separate. The integer codes COLLIDE: e.g. `8` = FILE_CHECKING in CurrentStatus but STOPED in PrintInfo.Status; `9` = HOMING vs COMPLETE.
The idle gate keys off CurrentStatus == 0 (IDLE) — that is the safe-to-write / safe-to-probe signal.
PrintInfo.Status is the real job lifecycle. Codes 2/3/4 (DROPPING/EXPOSURING/LIFTING) are resin-only leftovers that never fire on FDM.
A code outside the known range (newer firmware) normalizes to `unknown` while preserving the raw value — NEVER to `error`.
Normalized vocabulary: standby | preheating | printing | paused | resuming | cancelled | complete | error | unknown.

machine_status.unknown: other codes are busy/transient; treat as busy, never error

job_status.purpose: job sub-status — the real lifecycle signal

timing.RemainTime: does NOT exist — compute remaining = max(0, TotalTicks - CurrentTicks)
timing.Progress: 0..100 int, read VERBATIM (not derived from ticks); normalized as a time fraction
timing.envelope_TimeStamp: Unix MILLISECONDS — a different unit from the tick fields on the same frame

fans_read_only.note: read-only — no fan-set command on CC1
